_doc: Context rules for `aigis get --context <name>`. A rule declares that a specific pattern heading in a file applies only to the listed contexts. Patterns NOT listed here apply to every context. The filter removes a pattern block (header through next pattern-header or section break) when the requested context is not in the pattern's context list. Manifest-driven so we don't have to inline-tag every code block; can be evolved per Step 4's content rewrite.

_schema_rule.pattern_heading_prefix: heading text prefix at level 3 (### ...) to match
contexts: list of context names where this pattern applies

rules.reason: Injection pattern detection is most relevant when input comes from users (web API) or tool calls (agentic). Batch pipelines with vetted inputs often skip this.
Tool allowlist pattern only applies to agentic systems that expose tools/functions.
Approval gates for mutating operations apply only when the system executes actions, not when it only recommends.
Vector DB access control is RAG-specific.
Tenant isolation applies to RAG multi-tenant systems.
Permission inheritance on embeddings is RAG-specific.
Pre-embedding validation only applies to systems that ingest into a vector store or training pipeline.
Dataset versioning is primarily for fine-tuning / batch training pipelines.
Distribution monitoring applies to systems with embeddings or training datasets.
Batch rollback capability applies to ingestion / training pipelines.
Per-user rate limits apply primarily to user-facing APIs.
